speaker: charlie
text: Science time with some absolutely mind-blowing discoveries! First up, graphene's gone all superhero on us. <cite index='3-13,3-14'>Scientists have uncovered how graphene oxide hunts down and destroys harmful bacteria while leaving human cells completely unharmed, targeting a molecule found only in bacterial membranes.</cite> It's like having a microscopic security guard that only kicks out the troublemakers.

speaker: alice
text: That could be massive for fighting antibiotic-resistant superbugs! And speaking of game-changers, <cite index='3-1,3-8'>scientists have uncovered a surprising new picture of human origins that challenges the long-held idea of a single ancestral population in Africa.</cite> Turns out our family tree might be a bit more complicated than we thought!

speaker: charlie
text: Plus there's some intriguing research on fish oil that might make you rethink your supplements. <cite index='3-11,3-12'>Fish oil has long been praised as brain-boosting, but new research suggests that in people with repeated mild head injuries, a key omega-3 fatty acid in fish oil may actually interfere with recovery.</cite> Just goes to show - science keeps us on our toes, doesn't it?
